title: dianthus subacaulis subsp. cantabricus
intro: Dianthus subacaulis subsp. cantabricus, also known as the Cantabrian pink or Cantabrian catchfly, is a plant species in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Dianthus subacaulis subsp. cantabricus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made from its leaves and flowers, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or used topically in creams and ointments.
